question: How to create an accordion without JavaScript
description: Use the native <details> and <summary> elements to build fully functional, accessible accordions with zero JavaScript and no dependencies.

question: How to create a dropdown menu in HTML
description: Use the native <select> element for accessible, form-ready dropdown menus with no JavaScript required.

question: How to add a favicon to a website
description: Add favicons that look sharp on every device — from classic browser tabs to iOS home-screen icons and PWA manifests — with just a few <link> tags.

question: How to add form validation in HTML
description: Use HTML5 constraint attributes to validate user input before submission — no JavaScript required for common cases like required fields, email formats, and length limits.

question: How to add meta tags for SEO in HTML
description: Essential <meta> and <link> tags every page needs: charset, viewport, title, description, canonical, Open Graph, and Twitter Card.

question: How to create a modal dialog in HTML
description: The native <dialog> element provides accessible modals with built-in focus trapping, Escape key support, and a ::backdrop pseudo-element — no library needed.

question: How to add responsive images in HTML
description: Serve the right image size for every device using srcset, sizes, and the <picture> element — reducing bandwidth without sacrificing quality.

question: How to use semantic HTML5 elements
description: Replace generic <div> containers with semantic elements like <main>, <article>, <section>, and <aside> — improving accessibility, SEO, and code readability.

question: How to create a data table in HTML
description: Build accessible, well-structured data tables using semantic HTML elements like <thead>, <tbody>, <th scope>, and <caption>.

question: How to embed a video in HTML
description: Use the <video> element to embed self-hosted videos with multiple format fallbacks, poster images, and performance-friendly preload settings.
